characters.name: Aaron Hayden
characters.description: Aaron HaydenGenderMaleStatusAlive (Incarcerated)CrimeDouble HomicideVictimsPaula MacGoohan(Murder)Lynn Hayden(Murder)RelationshipsFamilyLynn Hayden(Sister: Deceased)Physical AppearanceHair colorDirty BlondeSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the Big GameLast appearanceMr. Monk and the Big GamePortrayed byEsteban Howell Aaron Haydenis the younger brother and murderer of coachLynn Hayden. A few years prior to his episode debut in "Mr. Monk and the Big Game", he murdered a woman namedPaula MacGoohan. He left DNA evidence all over the country club were the scene occurred, but the police had nothing to match it to, and thus he was never caught.

characters.name: Aaron Larkin
characters.description: Aaron LarkinGenderMaleStatusDeceasedCause of DeathBludgeoned with a wooden plank by Sally LarkinRelationshipsSignificant other(s)Sally Larkin(Wife)Series informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk Gets HypnotizedLast appearanceMr. Monk Gets HypnotizedPortrayed byHenry Czerny Aaron Larkinis the husband of actressSally Larkin. He was a billionaire philanthropist with a trophy wife who was accused of abusing his possibly kidnapping his wife. He was later killed by his wife, who staged a torturous kidnapping and daring escape.

characters.name: Aaron Polanski
characters.description: Aaron PolanskiAlso known asDr. PolanskiGenderMaleStatusAliveOccupationDermatologistInterestsStudying Skin DiseasesRelationshipsSignificant other(s)Natalie Teeger(Fling)Physical AppearanceHair colorBrownEye colorBrownSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the LeperLast appearanceMr. Monk and the LeperPortrayed byPaul Blackthorne Dr. Aaron Polanskiis a dermatologist whoMonksought advice from on possibly being contaminated with leprosy. He assured Monk that leprosy was almost never contagious and even hadRandy Disheras an acne patient when he was a teen. He then went on a date withNatalie Teegerand upon telling her about the time he had leprosy as a child, she freaked out to the point of bathing herself in hot water and Listerine. When Monk and Teeger were investigating the fact thatMandy Bronsonstaged her husband's so-called reappearance and killed the doppelganger, he arrived to help out Monk who fell off an air balloon and was hanging on for dear life on a cliffside over the Pacific. Polanski managed to save and was able to reconnect with Natalie.

characters.name: Abigail Carlyle
characters.description: Abigail CarlyleGenderFemaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyJacob Carlyle(Son)Daniel Carlyle(Son)EnemiesJanet NovakRaymond NovakPhysical AppearanceHair colorBrown (Greying)Series informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the KidLast appearanceMr. Monk and the KidPortrayed byBrooke Adams Abigail Carlyleis a wealthy socialite featured inSeason 3. Her youngest son,Daniel, a violinist for the Symphony, was kidnapped byJanetandRaymond Novak, held him for ransom, and cut off a pinky from his hand to state they meant business. She and her other son,Jacob, were warned not to contact the police at the threat of Daniel's life.Adrian Monk, who was investigating the severed pinkyTommy Graserfound figured out the problem the Carlyle's were having. Eventually theSFPDSWAT team breached the Novak residence, arresting the Novak couple and rescuing Daniel.

characters.name: Abigail Guthrie
characters.description: Mrs. Abigail Guthriebecame the personal assistant toArtemis Monk, the assayer to the small mining camp of Trouble, California. Shortly after the start of the Gold Rush, in 1849, Abigail reluctantly followed her husband, Hank, in his plans to sell their farm in Kansas and move to California to strike it rich. It never happened. Staking a small claim in the camp of Trouble, Hank worked himself, literally, to death with nothing to show for it. After severely weakening his constitution, he was finally carried off by rheumatic fever. Abigail had stood loyally by her husband for two years, but after his death, her options became limited. She had no money to return to Kansas, and no prospects of earning any, except to become a "sporting woman" (prostitute) in the local saloon. She tried to put off this choice by earning meager wages doing sewing and laundry for the miners, but her services were not much in demand - miners didn't see the point of spending hard-earned gold on clean clothes.

characters.name: Adam Kirk
characters.description: Adam KirkBiographical informationGenderMalePolitical informationRankLieutenantAllegiancesSan Francisco Police DepartmentOut of universe informationAppearances"Mr. Monk Goes to the Carnival"ActorStephen McHattie[Source] Adam Kirkwas a police officer with theSan Francisco Police Departmentfor over twenty years. Kirk was a longtime partner withLeland Stottlemeyer, prior to the latter's promotion to captain. Kirk suffered from a volatile temper, and over the course of his career, faced numerous accusations of police brutality and excessive force. On at least one of these occasions,Adrian Monk(at the time still on the police force) testified against him.

Biederbeck IIIWarrick TennysonHarold Krenshaw(former)Roderick Brody(former)Physical AppearanceHair colorDark brownEye colorDark brownSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the CandidateLast appearanceMr. Monk's Last Case: A Monk MoviePortrayed byTony Shalhoub(Present)Luiggi Debiasse (College)Grant Rosenmeyer (Teens)Aaron Wayne Hill (Pre-Teens)Aaron Linker (Younger) Former Detective MonkMr. PunctualityLarry TilburnFrank ConwayJerry LittleBen LincolnAdrian MelvilleFrank DePalmaCaptain CoolLeland RodríguezColumboSoft ServeAdj Monk is a very neurotic man with countless compulsions that nobody but him can make sense of, as well as being obsessively clean. He feels an overwhelming need for everything in his surroundings to be orderly and in its place, even down to every police officer in the area wearing matching socks, otherwise he cannot function. He also has a very comprehensive list of strange phobias, including (for unexplained reasons) milk. He is very petty and childish at times, especially when it comes to these phobias. Because of this, Monk often comes across as rather selfish and egotistical. He is also considered cheap, especially when it comes to money. He isn't an aggressive person nor does he react to violence well, but any misdeeds based on his wife would unleash something inside him. On some level, he is. But at his core, Adrian Monk is a man destroyed by grief. He has demonstrated on multiple occasions to actually have very low self-esteem, and he is unable to move past the death of his beloved wife, Trudy. It is his desire to solve her murder that fuels his determination to be mentally cleared to rejoin the police force. Of course, his badge number will have to be an even ten... Yet desp
characters.personality_section: Monk is a very neurotic man with countless compulsions that nobody but him can make sense of, as well as being obsessively clean. He feels an overwhelming need for everything in his surroundings to be orderly and in its place, even down to every police officer in the area wearing matching socks, otherwise he cannot function. He also has a very comprehensive list of strange phobias, including (for unexplained reasons) milk. He is very petty and childish at times, especially when it comes to these phobias. Because of this, Monk often comes across as rather selfish and egotistical. He is also considered cheap, especially when it comes to money. He isn't an aggressive person nor does he react to violence well, but any misdeeds based on his wife would unleash something inside him. On some level, he is. But at his core, Adrian Monk is a man destroyed by grief. He has demonstrated on multiple occasions to actually have very low self-esteem, and he is unable to move past the death of his beloved wife, Trudy. It is his desire to solve her murder that fuels his determination to be mentally cleared to rejoin the police force. Of course, his badge number will have to be an even ten... Yet despite his eccentricities, Monk is extremely intelligent and observant - perhapsbecauseof his manic attention to detail. These traits, combined with his deductive mind, make him one of the SFPD's best assets.

characters.name: Agnes Monk
characters.description: Agnes Monkwas the wife ofJack Monkand mother ofAmbroseandAdrian. Little is known about her apart from the fact that she was emotionally distant and had difficulty expressing affection towards her husband and sons. She did not like being hugged, preferring to shake hands instead. She was also something of a control freak, telling Adrian not to share his school lunch with anyone and to sit near the fire doors. She made her sons practice laughing to make them seem normal, but did not succeed. According to Adrian, she never even pretended to love his father, Jack. She once left a note in his lunchbox saying that "Your father is driving me crazy". Agnes died as the result of an unspecified illness in 1994. Her only appearance in the series was in a flashback in "Mr. Monk and Little Monk" where she was played by Rose Abdoo.

characters.name: Al Nicoletto
characters.description: Al NicolettoAlso known asAlan GruberAge40'sGenderMaleStatusAlive (Incarcarated)CrimeMurderVictimsRudy Schich(Murder)OccupationContract KillerInterestsWineRelationshipsEnemiesRudy SchichBen GruberAdrian MonkPhysical AppearanceHair colorBlackEye colorBrownSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk Gets DrunkLast appearanceMr. Monk Gets DrunkPortrayed byPaul Ben-Victor Al Nicolettois a character who appeared in "Mr. Monk Gets Drunk". Al Nicoletto was a professional hitman affiliated with the American Mafia. In 2005, a pair of mob accountants,Rudy SchichandBen Gruber, stole a large amount of money, leading their employers to sic Nicoletto on them to return it. Nicoletto tracks down Schich to a New York City hotel, where he murders him after unsuccessfully interrogating him for the location of Gruber, who had taken the loot with him to California. After finding a postcard with the Golden Gate Bridge in the room, Nicoletto lights a smoke, looking at his next destination.

characters.name: Alan Carnegie
characters.description: Alan Carnegiewas a judge murdered in 2009. Unknown to Carnegie, his wife,Rhonda, was carrying on an extra-marital affair with a man twenty years her junior. Knowing that there would be hell to pay if her husband ever found out, Rhonda decided to kill him rather than risk exposure and a messy divorce.

characters.name: Alby Drake
characters.description: Alby DrakeGenderMaleStatusDeceasedCause of DeathKilled by Winston BrennerOccupationExtremistInterestsProtestingSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the BlackoutLast appearanceMr. Monk and the BlackoutPortrayed byJudge Reinhold Alby Drakewas a character who appeared in the episode "Mr. Monk and the Blackout". Alby Drake wasBrenner’sformer best friend and college roommate. With it clear that Brenner faked his death nine years ago and has been hiding out ever since under an assumed name, they theorize that he probably is still in close contact with his friends.

characters.name: Alice Westergren
characters.description: Alice WestergrenAlso known asCorporal WestergrenGenderFemaleStatusAlive (Incarcerated)CrimeMurderVictimsTerry Chasen(Murder)RelationshipsSignificant other(s)Terry Chasen(Ex-Boyfriend: Deceased)EnemiesAdrian MonkLeland StottlemeyerRandy DisherNatalie TeegerTerry ChasenPhysical AppearanceHair colorBlondeSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the Secret SantaLast appearanceMr. Monk and the Secret SantaPortrayed byRachael Harris Corporal Alice Westergrenwas an officer of theSan Francisco Police Department. Unknown to anyone else in the department, Alice was having an affair with Detective Terry Chasen, which Alice took very seriously, while Chasen was estranged from his wife. Shortly before Christmas in 2005, Chasen reconciled with his wife, and told Alice their affair was over. Feeling her age, and unable to contemplate spending the rest of her life alone, Alice plotted to murder Chasen.

characters.description: Allison ClarkGenderFemaleStatusAlive (Incarcerated)CrimeDouble HomicideVictimsDewey Jordan(Murder, through Rob Sherman)Pam Sherman(Murder, through Rob Sherman)OccupationAssistant to Rob ShermanRelationshipsSignificant other(s)Rob Sherman(Lover)EnemiesPamela ShermanPhysical AppearanceHair colorBrownSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the Birds and the BeesLast appearanceMr. Monk and the Birds and the BeesPortrayed byStephanie Reibel Allison Clarkis an assistant and lover toRob Sherman. She was part of the part to murder his wife,Pamela. She was presumably arrested for her role in the double homicide although it's possible she cut a deal in exchange for her testimony against Sherman.

characters.name: Amanda Babbage
characters.description: Amanda BabbageFull nameAmanda BabbageGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedCause of DeathKilled in a bombing byBrian BabbageRelationshipsFamilyBrian Babbage(Brother)Ricky Babbage(Brother)Unnamed Father (Deceased)FriendsCarriePhysical AppearanceHair colorBlondeEye colorBlueSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the Sleeping SuspectLast appearanceMr. Monk and the Sleeping SuspectPortrayed byBeth Skipp Amanda Babbagewas the only daughter of three children, all of them potential heirs to their late father's very substantial fortune. Their father, either out of senility or just pure spite, changed his will constantly before his death, laying the ground for a very nasty legal battle between the three siblings, each of whom had enough to hire a battalion of lawyers. While Amanda and her younger brotherRickyeventually agreed to a settlement, their elder brotherBrianrefused, believing that he was entitled to a much larger share. In August, 2003, two months after moving into her new house, Amanda received a package that had been forwarded from her old address, supposedly from a bookseller. She absently opened the package, while talking on the phone with her lawyer, angrily telling her to appeal the court's latest ruling in favor of one of her brothers. Ironically, she complained about the length of the proceedings, and quipped that she would probably die before she saw any results. A few seconds later, she pried open the wrapper, and the package exploded, killing her instantly.

characters.name: Amber Post
characters.description: Amber PostGenderFemaleStatusAliveOccupationPorn StarRelationshipsFamilyUnnamed DaughterFriendsSharona FlemingEnemiesDexter LarsonPhysical AppearanceHair colorBlondeEye colorBlueSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk Meets the PlayboyLast appearanceMr. Monk Meets the PlayboyPortrayed byErinn Bartlett Amber Postis a character featured inSeason 2. She is a porn star forSapphire MagazinewhoDexter Larsonoffered a chance to be a centerfold for his magazine, with one twist, lied to theSFPD. She fulfilled her obligation but was guilt-ridden over the decision. AfterMonkand the SFPD discovered Larson's magnet, Post grew a conscience and told the police the truth.

characters.name: Ambrose Monk
characters.description: Ambrose MonkGenderMaleStatusAliveOccupationInstruction Manual WriterInterestsWritingReadingLanguagesBeyond EarthStaying indoorsRelationshipsFamilyJack Monk(father)Unnamed MotherAdrian Monk(brother)Jack Monk Jr.(half-brother)Trudy Monk(sister-in-law)Significant other(s)Yuki Nakamara(wife;Mr. Monk Gets Even)FriendsNatalie TeegerSharona FlemingRita van RankenEnemiesPat van RankenPhysical AppearanceHair colorBrownEye colorBrownSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the Three PiesLast appearanceMr. Monk's 100th CasePortrayed byJohn Turturro Ambrose Monkis the older brother ofAdrian Monk. Ambrose, like his brother Adrian, has severe OCD, which gives him a heightened attention to detail, deduction, and memory. Unfortunately, he suffers from a severe form of agoraphobia. As of 2003, he had not left the home he grew up in since 1971. Ambrose is highly intelligent and speaks multiple languages, using his literacy to write multi-lingual instruction manuals from the comfort of his home. Ambrose, like his brother Adrian, has severe OCD, which gives him a heightened attention to detail, deduction, and memory. Unfortunately, he suffers from a severe form of agoraphobia. As of 2003, he had not left the home he grew up in since 1971. Ambrose is highly intelligent and speaks multiple languages, using his literacy to write multi-lingual instruction manuals from the comfort of his home. Ambrose keeps all incoming mail addressed to their father, who left them when they were young, and he also keeps copies of the daily newspaper. He expects their father to return at any time and want to see these items. Ambrose also keeps his father's study as it appeared on the day the father left the family. Ambrose also has a very hot temper, which comes out when he gets angry with his brother Adrian. Ambrose is not angry with his father like Adrian is, possibly because Ambrose blamed himself for Jack leaving. He also blamed himself for the death of Adrian's wife, which led to Ambrose not talking to Adrian for seven years. Ambrose is shown to have a crush on Natalie, asking her out on a date when he met her.
characters.personality_section: Ambrose, like his brother Adrian, has severe OCD, which gives him a heightened attention to detail, deduction, and memory. Unfortunately, he suffers from a severe form of agoraphobia. As of 2003, he had not left the home he grew up in since 1971. Ambrose is highly intelligent and speaks multiple languages, using his literacy to write multi-lingual instruction manuals from the comfort of his home. Ambrose keeps all incoming mail addressed to their father, who left them when they were young, and he also keeps copies of the daily newspaper. He expects their father to return at any time and want to see these items. Ambrose also keeps his father's study as it appeared on the day the father left the family. Ambrose also has a very hot temper, which comes out when he gets angry with his brother Adrian. Ambrose is not angry with his father like Adrian is, possibly because Ambrose blamed himself for Jack leaving. He also blamed himself for the death of Adrian's wife, which led to Ambrose not talking to Adrian for seven years. Ambrose is shown to have a crush on Natalie, asking her out on a date when he met her.

characters.name: Angela Dirks
characters.description: Angela DirksGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedOccupationInterior DesignerRelationshipsFriendsWarren KempSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk Goes to the OfficeLast appearanceMr. Monk Goes to the OfficePortrayed byMeredith Scott Lynn Angela Dirksis a victim featured inSeason 4. She is an interior decorator working personally forWarren Kempand was hired to build curtains for his office window. She was killed by a man lipreading Kemp from the restaurant.

characters.name: Angeline Dilworth
characters.description: Angeline DilworthGenderFemaleStatusAlive (Incarcerated)CrimeMurderAttempted MurderVictimsRobert Boyd(Murder)Natalie Teeger(Attempted Murder)OccupationParamedicER NurseInterestsMoneyVoodooRelationshipsFamilyRobert Boyd(Uncle)Physical AppearanceHair colorBlondeSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the Voodoo CurseLast appearanceMr. Monk and the Voodoo CursePortrayed byClaire Rankin Angeline Dilworthis a paramedic and the niece of deceased milionaire Robert Byrd conceived of a superstitious parent. Angeline was first shown grieving over the death of her uncle, Robert Byrd, at her home, and whenAdrian Monkinterviewed her and noticed the good luck charms, Angeline stated that her uncle was largely superstitious. She later resurfaced atNatalie Teeger's home when Natalie fell ill after an attempted exorcism, and it was there that Monk recognized Angeline, though she denied seeing him before.

characters.name: Angie DeLuca
characters.description: Angie Delucais Sydney Teal ex-girlfriend back in college. To impress her, Sydney orchestrated a fake mugging and fend the fake mugger off to impress her with his heroism. She broke up with him later on. When Sydney became a billionaire computer magnate, Angie expressed her regret for breaking up with him and wished she married him back then to enjoy a luxurious life.

characters.name: Anna Pollard
characters.description: Anna PollardGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedCause of DeathForce Drowned byJay BennettOccupationSchool TeacherRelationshipsSignificant other(s)Eric Pollard(Husband)Jay Bennett(Lover)Physical AppearanceHair colorBrownEye colorBrownSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk, Private EyeLast appearanceMr. Monk, Private EyePortrayed byFrancesca Vannucci Anna Pollardis a victim inSeason 5. She is a school teacher who was having an affair with Dr.Jay Bennett. On his birthday when see decided to break it off with him on his yacht and chose to return to her husband, Eric, Bennett knocked her out with a champagne bottle, locked her in a crate with holes, and drown her in the San Francisco Bay.

characters.name: Ansara Waingaya
characters.description: Ansara WaingayaGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedCause of DeathKilled in a Hit-and-Run by Kenneth NicholsRelationshipsSignificant other(s)Samuel Waingaya(Husband)Physical AppearanceHair colorBlackEye colorBrownSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the Foreign ManLast appearanceMr. Monk and the Foreign ManPortrayed byConstance Ejuma Ansara Waingayais a character featured inSeason 8. She was a Nigerian woman and the wife ofSamuel Waingayawho was in San Francisco for a short time. After buying groceries at a store nearAdrian Monk'shome, she was killed in a hit-and-run byKenneth Nichols.

characters.name: Archie Modine
characters.description: Archie ModineAlso known asOfficer Modine (Formerly)GenderMaleStatusAlive (Incarcerated)CrimeMurderAttempted MurderVictimsSidney Teal(Murder)Adrian Monk (Attempted Murder)OccupationPolice Officer (Formerly)RelationshipsSignificant other(s)Myra Teal(Lover)FriendsSidney Teal(Formerly)EnemiesSidney TealPhysical AppearanceHair colorBlackSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the Billionaire MuggerLast appearanceMr. Monk and the Billionaire MuggerPortrayed byPeter Onorati Archie Modinewas a former cop who appeared in "Mr. Monk and the Billionaire Mugger". He is portrayed by Peter Onorati. In his college days, Archie colluded with his fraternity brother Sidney Teal to fake a mugging on Sidney's date with a woman named Angie DeLuca. Modine acted as a mugger that Sidney managed to "fight off". However, the relationship between Sidney and Angie didn't work out, as he later married a woman named Myra, and formed a very successful computer company.

characters.name: Arleen Cassidy
characters.description: Arleen CassidyStatusAliveGenderFemaleFamily?FriendsTrudy MonkBeth LandowEnemies?OccupationHeadmistress atAshton Preparatory SchoolInterestsAcademicsAppearancesMr. Monk Goes Back to SchoolPortrayed ByRosalind Chao Arleen CassidywasTrudy Monk's best friend in high school. They both attendedAshton Preparatory School, where, according to her, Trudy would help Arleen through English Lit and Arleen would help Trudy through math. Arleen later became headmaster of the school and hiredAdrian Monkto investigate the mysterious death of one of her teachers,Beth Landow.

characters.name: Arlene Boras
characters.description: Arlene BorasGenderFemaleStatusAlive (Incarcerated)CrimeMurderVictimsVickie DelineOccupationX-ray technicianInterestsMoneyRelationshipsEnemiesVickie DelinePeter MagneriPhysical AppearanceHair colorBlondeEye colorGreenSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk and the Naked ManLast appearanceMr. Monk's 100th CasePortrayed byAngela Kinsey Arlene Borasis a character featured inSeason 6. She was an X-ray technician at St. Andrews Hospital, where billionaire tech CEOPeter Magneriwas a patient. She discovered an aortic aneurysm during his X-ray, a very serious condition; if he experienced any excitement or stress then he was "a heart attack waiting to happen". Rather than warn him, she chose to get rich off the ultimate piece of insider information. If Magneri died, his company would be doomed. She swapped out Magneri's real X-ray with another, making him think he had a perfect bill of health. Not even his doctor knew how sick he really was. Shortly after, she quit her job and began day-trading, playing the stock market, short selling and placing bets against Magneri's company.

characters.name: Barbara O'Keefe
characters.description: Barbara O'KeefeGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedCause of DeathDrowned by Xavier DankoInterestsDaily BathsRelationshipsFriendsDr. BellHer pet catPhysical AppearanceHair colorBlondeSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk Goes to Group TherapyLast appearanceMr. Monk Goes to Group TherapyPortrayed byJoelle Carter Barbara O'Keefeis a character featured inSeason 8. She was a patient of Dr.Neven Bell's. In the middle of a drought, she took her third bath of the day and chatted with her mother about Dr. Bell over the phone. The call was abruptly cut off and her cat entered the bathroom. She realized someone must have let the cat in so she got out of the tub and wrapped a towel around her body. Unfortunately for her, an intruder broke into the bathroom and drowned her in the tub. He then dressed her in a bikini and dumped her in a pool near her apartment. She was found the next morning by the pool cleaner floating in the pool and wearing a bikini. Her killer was later revealed to beXavier Danko, a former member of her therapy group. Danko had come to Dr. Bell after developing an obsession with an exotic dancer by the name of Tiffany Bolt, and left the group claiming to be cured. In reality,  he had murdered Tiffany, stuffed her body in the trunk of her car, and drove it into the reservoir. When San Francisco went through a drought, he feared the car and body would eventually be found, and thus he tried to kill off the other people of his therapy group so they could not connect him to it.

characters.name: Ben Glaser
characters.description: Ben GlaserGenderMaleStatusAlive (Incarcerated)CrimeMurderEmbezzlementVictimsKenneth Woods(Murder)OccupationCo-owner of Tiger Bay TruckingInterestsSaving Money via Using Recycled Parts in TrucksRelationshipsEnemiesKenneth WoodsPhysical AppearanceHair colorBlondeEye colorBlueSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk Meets His DadLast appearanceMr. Monk Meets His DadPortrayed byBrian Kerwin Ben Glaseris a character featured inSeason 5. He is the owner of Tiger Bay Trucking Company, a big rig hauling organization in Texas. He borrowedJack Monkstruck and was confronted by his partner,Kenneth Woods, being accused of embezzling money from the company by using recycled parts in big rigs which would endanger their employees. When Kenneth got his tie caught in the engine and strangling him, Ben used that opportunity to kill Woods. To cover up his role in Kenneth's death, he had Jack take various routes to certain cities without using the 402 with a fib about the road being under construction in order to get the GPS odometer to 5,000, erasing any memory that Ben was at Kenneth's estate. When a piece of Kenneth's ugly tie was discovered in Jack's rig, Ben was arrested for murder.

characters.name: Ben Gruber
characters.description: Ben GruberAlso known asLarry ZwibellGenderMaleStatusDeceasedCause of DeathHeart AttackOccupationAccountantInterestsMoneyWineRelationshipsFriendsAdrian MonkRudy SchichEnemiesAl NicolettoSeries informationFirst appearanceMr. Monk Gets DrunkLast appearanceMr. Monk Gets DrunkPortrayed byDaniel Roebuck Ben Gruberis a victim featured inSeason 4. He was an accountant back in New York who, alongsideRudy Schick, stole over 3 million dollars from the mafia. His partner, Rudy, was whacked in New York byAl Nicolettowhile Ben was hiding out in Sonoma County, California under the alias, Larry Zwibbel. While there, he died of a heart attack in his room. His body and the money he stole was found bySylvia Willis, Dr.David Sobin,CalandHeidi Gefsky,Pierre LaCoste, and butler Ricardo, all of whom agreed to split the money and clear any trace of Gruber being there. His body was later discovered in a cask of wine.
